seo.metaDescription: 8小時濟州郵輪岸上觀光大巴之旅，依靠港安排路線：濟州港走東部UNESCO世界遺產路線，江汀港走漢拿山＋偶來市場路線。
seo.title: 濟州郵輪岸上觀光・大型旅遊巴 | AtoC Korea
seo.description: 濟州郵輪岸上大型旅遊巴之旅：濟州港走東部UNESCO世界遺產路線；江汀港走南部路線。含所有門票、持證導遊，並於船隻啟航前提前返回碼頭。

catalog_card.title: 濟州郵輪岸上觀光：旅遊巴之旅
catalog_card.subtitle: 依靠港安排的郵輪一日遊，提供濟州港及江汀港兩種路線選擇
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: 本行程為大型旅遊巴郵輪岸上觀光，依您的靠港地點彈性調整路線。濟州港（北部）走途經城山日出峰的東海岸UNESCO世界遺產路線；江汀港（南部）走漢拿山1100高地＋南海岸地質景觀＋偶來市場路線。兩條路線均預留充裕緩衝時間返回郵輪碼頭。

hero.tagline: 選擇您的靠港地點：濟州港涵蓋東部UNESCO世界遺產路線；江汀港涵蓋漢拿山＋偶來市場路線。同樣的大型旅遊巴形式，同樣的8小時行程——只是島上精選景點各有不同。

itineraryStops.name: 濟州郵輪碼頭接送（濟州港 — 北部）
itineraryStops.description:
您的司機與英語導遊將在**濟州國際旅客碼頭**（제주항 국제여객터미널，地址：濟州市臨港路191號 — 聯合國/LOCODE **KRCHA**）迎接您的一行人，這是**2015年**開放接待國際郵輪的北海岸郵輪港口。

碼頭設有兩個泊位——**主泊位（360公尺，水深12公尺，14萬噸容量）**與**緊急泊位（306公尺，水深11.5公尺，8.5萬噸容量）**——每次**僅接待一艘郵輪**。接送時間定於**您的船隻公告清關後30分鐘**，以應對多船同時靠港時的入境審查時間差。

司機持有英韓文姓名牌、行程卡（含所有停靠時間及緊急叫車用韓文地址），以及營運商的24小時聯絡電話。車輛停放於入境大廳旁**可容納100輛巴士的停車場**。

上車前，導遊將說明當日行程、集合時間、天氣狀況及任何靠港當日的調整事項。若旅客需要換匯（濟州銀行）、購買SIM卡／租借Wi-Fi，或使用多語言旅遊資訊中心，導遊將全程陪同——這些服務皆設於碼頭內。

根據**韓國文化體育觀光部**資料，**2024年共有81萬名郵輪旅客乘坐414艘船隻抵港**——幾乎是前一年的三倍——因此多船靠港日確實可能出現碼頭壅塞情形。
highlights: **清關後30分鐘接送** — 緩衝時間用於應對入境延誤
司機及英語導遊持姓名牌於入境大廳迎接
**主泊位 360公尺 × 12公尺 ／ 14萬噸容量**（同時僅停靠一艘船隻）
**100輛巴士停車容量**緊鄰碼頭 — 快速登車
旅遊資訊中心、濟州銀行換匯、ATM、免費Wi-Fi一應俱全
附韓文地址行程卡，可作緊急叫車備用

sources: VisitKorea / english.visitkorea.or.kr (Jeju cruise terminal facilities + 100-bus parking)
South Korea Ministry of Oceans & Fisheries (terminal opening 2015; berth specifications)
Korea Cruise Port Sales / k-cruiseinfo.com (berth depths, tonnage capacity)
Ministry of Culture, Sports & Tourism (2024 cruise volume: 810,000 pax / 414 ships)
Jeju Provincial Government (Imhang-ro address, Geonip-dong; UN/LOCODE KRCHA)

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **濟州港（北部）是14萬總噸以下船隻的主要郵輪入口**——也是東線行程（城山／涉地可支／城邑）的天然出發點，三個主要景點均在碼頭60至80分鐘車程範圍內。對於**25至40人的巴士團**而言，清關後30分鐘的緩衝時間至關重要——大型團體下船速度較慢，多船同時靠港時入境排隊壅塞十分常見。濟州港的100輛巴士停車容量意味著巴士可直接停在**入境大廳門口**（無需等待接駁車），讓全日行程維持緊湊。若您的船隻停靠江汀（南部）而非此處，應選擇**本行程的南線方案**——從江汀到城山單程需70分鐘以上，將大幅佔用行程時間。

smartNotes.tip: 請於預定接送時間前5至10分鐘抵達；郵輪／非軍事區行程請攜帶附照片之身分證件

convenience.restroom: 於集合地點（地鐵站／飯店大廳／港口碼頭）
convenience.parking: 旅客免辦理 — 於車輛旁接送

itineraryStops.name: 城山日出峰
itineraryStops.category: 聯合國教科文組織凝灰岩火山錐 — 日出峰（三重聯合國教科文組織認定）
itineraryStops.description:
**城山日出峰（성산일출봉，「日出峰」）**是一座**有5,000年歷史的蘇爾特塞型水火山凝灰岩錐**——一種罕見地形，形成於海底玄武岩噴發衝破海面時，岩漿與海水相遇，蒸汽爆炸性火山灰堆積而成**182公尺的要塞形火山口**邊緣，而非典型的熔岩錐。

聯合國教科文組織正式將其定義為**「新興蘇爾特塞型火山構造的傑出典範」**。火山口**寬600公尺、深90公尺**，周圍環繞著**99塊鋸齒狀岩石**，當地傳說將其比喻為石製王冠。城山日出峰擁有**三重聯合國教科文組織認定**：**世界自然遺產（2007年7月2日列入）**，為**「濟州火山島與熔岩洞」**遺產地的一部分、**生物圈保護區（2002年）**及**全球地質公園（2010年10月）**——在韓國景點中，唯有漢拿山本身才享有同等殊榮。

海側山腳下鑿有**18座日本帝國二戰時期洞穴**（神風特攻隊自殺艇掩體，1945年）。步道：**單程0.8公里，爬升約140公尺，來回約60分鐘**，路面為寬闊石階。

**海女（해녀）表演**每日**13:30及15:00**在火山錐下方的岩石海灣舉行——濟州女性潛水員（2016年聯合國教科文組織非物質文化遺產）不使用水肺裝備下潛。

**2025年4月**更新票價：**成人₩5,000 ／ 青少年及兒童₩2,500 ／ 團體成人₩1,600**，7歲以下及65歲以上免費。山頂觀景台也是**瀛洲十景（濟州十大名景）**之一，至今仍是韓國最重要的**年度新年觀日出朝聖地**——每年1月1日黎明前有逾5萬名遊客登頂，自1995年起舉辦市級日出節慶活動。

**牛島（우도，「牛島」）**——韓國最大的次生火山島——從山頂清晰可見，位於海岸外4公里處，可從城山港搭乘**15分鐘渡輪**（來回₩10,500），適合在其他行程中有額外時間的旅客前往。
highlights: **三重聯合國教科文組織認定**：世界遺產 2007-07-02 ＋ 生物圈保護區 2002 ＋ 全球地質公園 2010-10
**距今5,000年** — 蘇爾特塞型水火山噴發，岩漿與海水相遇的蒸汽爆炸性誕生
火山口**寬600公尺×深90公尺**，環繞著**99塊鋸齒狀火口岩**（「石之王冠」）
**海拔182公尺** — 全長0.8公里／爬升約140公尺，石階步道來回約60分鐘
山腳設有**18座日本帝國二戰洞窟** — 1945年神風特攻自殺艇掩蔽所
海邊石灣每日14:00舉行**海女表演一場（惡劣天氣時可能取消）** — 2016年列入UNESCO非物質文化遺產
**每月第一個星期一休館**（不定期休息日）
timeUsed: 停車＋購票＋山腳說明 — 約10分鐘
登頂觀景台（0.8公里／爬升140公尺） — 約30分鐘
山頂景觀＋火口岩拍照 — 約20分鐘
下山 — 約20分鐘
山腳可選觀看海女表演（14:00場次；惡劣天氣時可能取消） — 約10分鐘
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **城山日出峰是東線行程最重要的自然景觀。** 這座凝灰岩錐體是濟州島UNESCO世界自然遺產景觀的一部分，讓郵輪旅客對島嶼火山海岸線留下深刻的第一印象。此站安排在行程前段，因為它是濟州最具代表性的景點之一，且沿火口岩的步道可依旅客體能狀況與天氣彈性調整。不想攀登的旅客可在山腳區域欣賞海景及拍照留念，其餘旅客則可繼續前往火山口觀景台。

sources: UNESCO WHC inscription 1264 (Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes, 2007-07-02)
UNESCO Global Geoparks Network — Jeju (2010-10 designation)
Cultural Heritage Administration — Natural Monument designation 2000-07-19
Seongsan Ilchulbong official site (seongsan.kr) — April 2025 admission update
Jeju Haenyeo Museum — UNESCO Intangible 2016 inscription record

smartNotes.tip: 全程石階但保養良好 — 便服即可輕鬆應對；設有休息站，適合全家出遊；日出時段深受歡迎，但黎明前已聚集大批人潮
smartNotes.photo: 晴天可在山頂西望漢拿山、北眺牛島；下山步道途中可俯瞰海女黑沙灘
smartNotes.facilities: 咖啡廳（Fritz Coffee距離1.3公里，是欣賞山峰外觀的最佳地點）；可從城山港（步行1.5公里／約15分鐘）搭乘渡輪前往牛島

convenience.restroom: 入口及山腳均設有服務設施

visitBasics.closed: 全年開放（惡劣天氣時可能臨時關閉）
visitBasics.admission: 成人5,000韓元／青少年及兒童2,500韓元（參考VisitKorea資料）
visitBasics.walking: 中等難度 — 穩步攀登石階約20–30分鐘（約600級台階）；每隔數分鐘設有休息椅

itineraryStops.name: 涉地可支（東岬）
itineraryStops.category: 火山岬角 — 安藤忠雄建築 + 防頭浦燈塔 + 韓劇拍攝地
itineraryStops.description:
**涉地可支（섭지코지）** 是濟州島東海岸一處狹長的火山岬角。在濟州方言中，**koji** 意指岬角或海岬，此地以開闊的海景、平緩的海岸步道、白色燈塔，以及回望城山日出峰的壯闊視野聞名。

這段步行風景優美，無需費力攀爬，非常適合希望拍攝精彩照片、享受清新海風，又不想耗費整天爬山的郵輪旅客。視時間而定，旅客或許還能參觀玻璃屋區域及由民美術館外觀，兩者均與建築師安藤忠雄有關。

此行程保持彈性安排，因為風速、降雨、展館開放時間及團體步行速度均可能影響岬角的遊覽範圍。
highlights: **2.1 公里岬角步道**（約 30 分鐘，爬升僅 14 公尺）— 部分路段適合輪椅／嬰兒車通行

**防頭浦燈塔** — 八角形，**每 4 秒閃爍一次**，矗立於불근오름（紅色火山錐）頂端 > **Notes:** File paths (lines 1, 2, 23) are left unchanged as technical values. Korean proper noun 불근오름 in line 25 is retained with a parenthetical translation.
**協子煙臺烽火台** — 朝鮮時代煙臺，規模 4 公尺 × 9 公尺，**特別紀念物第 23-2 號**
**安藤忠雄建築** — Glass House 餐廳 + 由民美術館（菲尼克斯島）
**禪岩（선바위）** — 距岸 10 公尺的火山岩頸，相傳為龍王么子化身
**戲劇拍攝地**：《All In》（2003）、《我的女孩》（2005）等多部作品
**油菜花季 3–4 月**（金黃山坡）；**繡球花 6 月**（藍紫色花海）
**海岬免費入場**；由民美術館 + Glass House 單獨售票
城山日出峰**清晰可見，位於海岬頂端以北 4 公里**——絕佳的三重聯合國教科文組織世界遺產全景背景
timeUsed: 停車 + 入場 — 約 5 分鐘
海岬步道健行 — 約 25 分鐘
燈塔 + 協子煙臺拍照 — 約 10 分鐘
安藤忠雄建築外觀欣賞 — 約 10 分鐘
返回遊覽車 + 緩衝時間 — 約 10 分鐘
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **涉地可支在城山之後，加入一段輕鬆的海岸漫步路段。** 此海岬讓旅客從另一個角度欣賞濟州東部：海蝕崖、燈塔景觀、開闊草地，以及海岸線旁的當代建築。此站安排於此，因為它鄰近城山，可在午餐前有效率地完成上午行程。停留具彈性，導遊可視風況、天氣及郵輪當日時間靈活調整步行長短。

VisitJeju — Seopjikoji official listing

convenience.restroom: 停車場及亞쿠아星球區域均設有洗手間

itineraryStops.name: 午餐（濟州東部 — 當地海鮮或黑豬肉）
itineraryStops.description:
**濟州東部午餐站**，位於城山／城邑／表善地區由導遊精選的當地餐廳，依郵輪一日遊行程時間妥善安排。

**兩種典型選項**，依團體偏好及供應情況選擇：**(1) 濟州黑豬烤肉**（제주 흑돼지）— 濟州的代表食材，本土黑豬於2015年被列為**韓國天然紀念物**，豬五花在桌邊以炭火燒烤，搭配傳統小菜享用；或 **(2) 갈치조림**（갈치조림 — 燉白帶魚配白蘿蔔、醬油辣椒醬）— 濟州東部著名海鮮料理，食材來自城山及城邑地區港口的每日新鮮漁獲。

大多數餐廳提供素食選項，需提前告知（導遊約於用餐前30分鐘與餐廳確認）。午餐時間約**60分鐘**，符合郵輪一日遊緊湊的行程安排（全天通常為港口往返8小時）。飲料：大麥茶（免費）、當地**濟州啤酒**（約₩6,000）或**漢拏山燒酒**（濟州特產蒸餾酒，約₩5,000）。

輪換餐廳均接受**主要信用卡（Visa／Mastercard）及現金韓元**；郵輪旅遊認證場所標配英中雙語圖文菜單。午餐**不含**於標準郵輪旅遊定價中 — 通常自費**每人₩15,000–25,000**（依餐點而異）；預訂時可選擇含午餐升級套餐。
highlights: 導遊精選濟州東部餐廳 — 城山／城邑／表善地區
**濟州黑毛豬烤肉**（2015年天然紀念物）或**갈치조림**（紅燒帶魚）
約60分鐘——適合郵輪當日行程的時間安排
中英雙語圖文菜單，可刷卡及付現
提前告知可提供素食選項
**午餐費用不含於行程中**——需自費₩15,000–25,000／人（可升級加購）

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **午餐安排在東線行程的中段附近**，以在城山和涉地可支之後維持全天順暢節奏。位於城山、城邑或表善地區的餐廳，能讓行程更輕鬆地銜接下午的村莊景點，無需大幅繞路。導遊將依據團體人數、餐廳營業狀況及時間安排，選擇合適的當地餐廳。若郵輪入境手續、交通或上午拍照花費的時間超出預期，此安排亦可提供實際的緩衝空間。

itineraryStops.name: 城邑民俗村
itineraryStops.category: 朝鮮時代城邑古鎮——重要民俗文化遺產（建於1423年）
itineraryStops.description:
**城邑民俗村（성읍민속마을）**是一座至今仍有居民生活的朝鮮時代村落，完整保存了濟州古老的石牆、茅草屋頂房屋、狹窄小巷及傳統民居格局。歷史上，該村曾是旌義縣的行政中心，旌義縣為朝鮮時代濟州主要地方行政區之一，其文化遺產價值已獲列為重要民俗文化遺產。

與刻意重建的民俗公園不同，這裡至今仍有居民居住，因此參觀時務必在民居附近保持尊重與安靜。村落中央以兩棵古老的榉樹與朴樹聞名，此外亦保留有傳統濟州民居文化、石敢當（石爺爺）雕像，以及與村莊生活相關的在地故事。
highlights: **建於1423年**，由世宗大王敕建——行政中心功能延續至**1914年（約500年）**
原邑城城廓範圍內保存有**110棟傳統建築**
**韓國重要民俗文化遺產**（全村及多棟建築均列入保護）
村落中央有**兩棵樹齡約600年的古樹**：光葉榉與朴樹——均列為**天然紀念物**
依**朝鮮時代風水（堪輿）原則**規劃設計——典型縣城格局
**《大長今》（MBC 2003–2004）**拍攝地——享譽國際的韓劇經典名作
**오메기술（Omegisul）** — 由單一家族世代釀造的濟州米酒（免費品嚐）
歷史傷痕：**1826年大火**摧毀約80棟建築；**1948年濟州四三事件**造成的破壞
**免費入場** — 村內仍有約250戶家庭居住，延續傳統
timeUsed: 停車＋村莊解說員歡迎簡介 — 約10分鐘
步行前往古樹及村中心 — 約10分鐘
參觀韓屋內部（2至3間代表性民居）— 約15分鐘
오메기술釀酒坊免費品嚐 — 約10分鐘
偶來小巷漫步＋拍照攝影 — 約10分鐘
緩衝時間 — 約5分鐘
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **城邑民俗村是東線行程的主要文化亮點。** 它將上午的火山景觀與濟州的人文歷史融為一體：石牆、茅草屋、村莊古樹、老巷弄與在地故事。平坦的地形非常適合團體巴士遊覽，在返回港口前，讓旅客以更靜謐的方式深度體驗傳統濟州生活。

itineraryStops.name: 返回濟州郵輪碼頭（濟州港 — 北部）
itineraryStops.description:
驅車返回位於濟州市北岸的**濟州國際旅客碼頭**（제주항 국제여객터미널）。從濟州東部（城山／城邑地區）出發，經1132號公路（海岸環形公路）及16號公路，車程約70至80分鐘，途中設有一個指定休息站。抵達時間經過精心安排，確保賓客在**船班全員登船時間前至少60分鐘**抵達碼頭，符合郵輪業登船排隊所需的標準緩衝時間。

車輛駛入**可容納100輛巴士的停車場**，賓客在碼頭入口下車，由司機／導遊協助搬運行李。若賓客需搭乘濟州國際機場的傍晚航班，**100號巴士可從郵輪碼頭直達機場** — 導遊可就此選項提供建議。

碼頭內設有便利商店、藥局、小吃店、販售**免稅商品**的紀念品店、可供海外卡使用的濟州銀行ATM、外幣兌換服務（一般營業時間08:00–17:00），以及SK電信T-mobile Wi-Fi租用櫃台 — 適合賓客在出航前進行最後採購或補充行動數據。

另備有**前往東門傳統市場的免費接駁巴士**（濟州市歷史最悠久、規模最大的市場，車程約10分鐘），供登船前尚有充裕時間的賓客搭乘。
highlights: **在全員登船時間前60分鐘以上下車** — 郵輪登船標準緩衝時間

stops.name: Jeju Cruise Terminal Pickup (Jeju Port — north)
stops.description: Your driver and English-speaking guide meet your party at the **Jeju International Passenger Terminal** (제주항 국제여객터미널, address: 191 Imhang-ro, Jeju-si — UN/LOCODE **KRCHA**), the northern coast cruise port that opened in **2015** to receive international cruise vessels. The terminal has two berths — the **Main Berth (360 m, 12 m water depth, 140,000-ton capacity)** and an **Emergency Berth (306 m, 11.5 m water depth, 85,000-ton capacity)** — handling **one cruise ship at a time**. Pickup is timed for **30 minutes after your ship's posted clearance**, absorbing immigration variance during multi-ship arrivals. The driver carries an English/Korean name placard, a printed itinerary card (with all stop times and Korean-script addresses for emergency taxi use), and the operator's 24/7 contact number. Vehicle is parked in the **100-bus parking capacity lot** adjacent to the arrival hall. Before boarding, the guide reviews the day's flow, all-aboard time, weather, and any port-day adjustments. If your guests need currency exchange (Jeju Bank), a SIM/Wi-Fi rental, or use of the multilingual tourist information center, the guide accompanies — these are all on-site within the terminal. Per **South Korea's Ministry of Culture, Sports & Tourism**, **810,000 cruise tourists arrived on 414 ships in 2024** — nearly tripling the prior year — so terminal congestion is realistic on multi-ship days.
highlights: **Pickup 30 min after ship clearance** — buffer absorbs immigration delays
Driver + English-speaking guide meet at arrival hall with name placard
**Main Berth 360 m × 12 m / 140,000-ton capacity** (single concurrent ship)
**100-bus parking** capacity adjacent to terminal — fast vehicle boarding
Tourist info center, Jeju Bank currency exchange, ATMs, free Wi-Fi on-site
Itinerary card with Korean-script addresses provided as emergency-taxi backup

sources: VisitKorea / english.visitkorea.or.kr (Jeju cruise terminal facilities + 100-bus parking)
South Korea Ministry of Oceans & Fisheries (terminal opening 2015; berth specifications)
Korea Cruise Port Sales / k-cruiseinfo.com (berth depths, tonnage capacity)
Ministry of Culture, Sports & Tourism (2024 cruise volume: 810,000 pax / 414 ships)

stops.whyOnRoute: **Jeju Port (north) is the primary cruise gateway** for vessels under 140,000 GT — and the natural origin point for the east-route circuit (Seongsan / Seopjikoji / Seongeup), since all three core POIs sit within a 60–80 minute drive of this terminal. For a **bus tour with 25–40 pax**, the 30-minute post-clearance buffer is essential — large groups disembark slowly and immigration backups are common during multi-ship arrivals. The 100-bus parking capacity at Jeju Port also means the coach can be **right at the arrival hall door** (no shuttle wait), keeping the day's schedule tight. If your ship docks at Gangjeong (south) instead of here, the **southern route option of this tour** is the correct match — the drive from Gangjeong to Seongsan would consume 70+ minutes one-way, eating the day.

stops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong
stops.category: UNESCO Tuff Cone — Sunrise Peak (Triple UNESCO Designation)
stops.description: **Seongsan Ilchulbong (성산일출봉, 'Sunrise Peak')** is a **5,000-year-old Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic tuff cone** — a rare landform created when an underwater basalt eruption broke the sea surface, the magma met seawater, and the resulting steam-explosive ash built a **182 m fortress-shaped crater** rim instead of a typical lava cone. The result is what UNESCO formally calls **'an exceptional example of an emergent Surtseyan-type volcanic edifice'**. The crater is **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** that local lore likens to a stone crown. Seongsan carries the **Triple UNESCO designation**: **World Natural Heritage (inscribed 2007-07-02)** as part of the **'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' property**, **Biosphere Reserve (2002)**, and **Global Geopark (2010-10)** — a stack matched only by Hallasan itself among Korean sites. **18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** (kamikaze suicide-boat shelters, 1945) are tunneled into the seaward base. Trail: **0.8 km one-way, ~140 m elevation gain, 60 min round trip** on wide stone steps. **Haenyeo (해녀) demonstrations** run **daily at 13:30 and 15:00** at the rocky cove below the cone — Jeju women divers (UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage 2016) descend without scuba gear. Admission updated **April 2025**: **Adult ₩5,000 / Youth+Child ₩2,500 / Group adult ₩1,600**, free under 7 and over 65. The summit deck is also part of the **Yeongjusipgyeong (Jeju 10 Scenic Wonders) canon**, and remains Korea's premier **annual New Year sunrise pilgrimage** destination — 50,000+ visitors ascend before dawn on January 1, with municipal sunrise-festival programming since 1995. **Udo Island (우도, 'Cow Island')** — Korea's largest secondary volcanic island — is clearly visible 4 km offshore from the summit, accessible by **15-min ferry from Seongsan Port** (₩10,500 round-trip) for guests with extra time on a different itinerary.
highlights: **Triple UNESCO**: World Heritage 2007-07-02 + Biosphere Reserve 2002 + Global Geopark 2010-10
**5,000 years old** — Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic eruption, magma-meets-seawater steam-explosive birth
Crater **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** (the 'stone crown')
**182 m above sea level** — 0.8 km / ~140 m gain, 60-min round trip on stone-step trail
**18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** at base — 1945 kamikaze suicide-boat shelters
**Haenyeo demonstration once daily at 14:00 (subject to bad-weather cancellation)** at the seaside cove — UNESCO Intangible 2016
**Admission updated April 2025**: Adult ₩5,000 / Youth ₩2,500 / FREE under 7 + over 65
Spring/autumn hours **05:00–19:00** (last entry 18:20)
**Closes for first Monday of each month** (occasional rest day)
timeUsed: Parking + ticket + base brief — ~10 min
Ascent to summit deck (0.8 km / 140 m gain) — ~30 min
Summit views + crater rim photos — ~20 min
Descent — ~20 min
Optional haenyeo viewing at base (14:00 show; may be canceled in bad weather) — ~10 min
stops.whyOnRoute: **Seongsan Ilchulbong is the main natural highlight of the east route.** The tuff cone is part of Jeju's UNESCO World Natural Heritage landscape and gives cruise guests a powerful first look at the island's volcanic coastline. The stop is placed early because it is one of Jeju's most recognizable sights and because the rim walk can be adjusted to guest fitness and weather. Guests who prefer not to climb can enjoy the base area, sea views, and photo points while others continue toward the crater viewpoint.

Cultural Heritage Administration — Natural Monument designation 2000-07-19
Seongsan Ilchulbong official site (seongsan.kr) — April 2025 admission update

stops.name: Seopjikoji (East Cape)
stops.category: Volcanic Cape — Tadao Ando Architecture + Bangdupo Lighthouse + Drama Filming
stops.description: **Seopjikoji (섭지코지)** is a narrow volcanic cape on Jeju's east coast. In Jeju dialect, **koji** means a cape or headland, and the place is known for open sea views, a gentle coastal path, the white lighthouse, and the broad view back toward Seongsan Ilchulbong. The walk is scenic without being a heavy hike, so it works well for cruise guests who want strong photos and fresh sea air without spending the whole day climbing. Depending on time, guests may also see the Glass House area and the Yumin Art Museum exterior, both associated with architect Tadao Ando. The visit is kept flexible because wind, rain, gallery hours, and group walking speed can affect how much of the cape is covered.
highlights: **2.1 km cape trail** (~30 min, only 14 m gain) — wheelchair/stroller-friendly stretches
**Bangdupo Lighthouse** — octagonal, **flashes every 4 sec**, atop Bulgeun Oreum red volcanic cone
**Hyeopja Yeondae Beacon Fire Station** — 4 m × 9 m Joseon yeondae, **Special Monument No. 23-2**
**Tadao Ando architecture** — Glass House restaurant + Yumin Art Gallery (Phoenix Island)
**Seonbawi (Seon Rock)** — 10 m offshore volcanic neck, dragon king's youngest son legend
**Drama filming**: 'All In' (2003), 'My Girl' (2005), and others
**Canola season March–April** (yellow slopes); **hydrangea June** (blue/violet)
**FREE cape admission**; Yumin Art Gallery + Glass House separately ticketed
Seongsan Ilchulbong **clearly visible 4 km north** from the cape tip — perfect Triple-UNESCO panorama backdrop
timeUsed: Parking + entrance — ~5 min
Cape trail walk — ~25 min
Lighthouse + Hyeopja Yeondae photos — ~10 min
Tadao Ando buildings exterior viewing — ~10 min
Return to coach + buffer — ~10 min
stops.whyOnRoute: **Seopjikoji adds an easy coastal-walk section after Seongsan.** The cape gives guests a different angle on eastern Jeju: sea cliffs, lighthouse views, open grassland, and contemporary architecture near the coastline. It is placed here because it is close to Seongsan and keeps the morning route efficient before lunch. The stop is scenic but flexible, so the guide can shorten or extend the walk depending on wind, weather, and cruise-day timing.

stops.name: Lunch (East Jeju — local seafood or black pork)
stops.description: **East Jeju lunch stop** at a guide-vetted local restaurant in the Seongsan / Seongeup / Pyoseon area, chosen to fit cruise day-trip timing. **Two typical options**, selected based on group preference and availability: **(1) Jeju Black Pork Heukdwaeji BBQ** (제주 흑돼지) — Jeju's signature ingredient, the indigenous Black Pig was designated **Natural Monument of Korea** in 2015, and pork-belly cuts are grilled over charcoal at the table with traditional banchan; or **(2) Galchi Jorim** (갈치조림 — braised hairtail with radish, soy-chili sauce) — eastern Jeju's famous seafood dish, drawing on the daily catch from Seongsan and Seongeup-area harbors. Vegetarian options are available at most venues with advance notice (the guide confirms with the restaurant ~30 min ahead). Lunch duration is **~60 minutes**, fitting the cruise day-trip's tight timeline (full day usually 8 hours port-to-port). Beverages: barley tea (free), local **Jeju Beer** (~₩6,000), or **Hallasan Soju** (Jeju's signature distilled spirit, ~₩5,000). All restaurants in the rotation accept **major cards (Visa/Mastercard) + cash KRW**; bilingual photo menus (English/Chinese) are standard at the cruise-tour-vetted venues. Lunch is **NOT included** in the standard cruise tour pricing — typical out-of-pocket is **₩15,000–25,000 per person** (varies by dish); inclusive lunch upgrade available on booking.

stops.name: Seongeup Folk Village
stops.category: Joseon-era Walled Town — Important Folklore Cultural Heritage (1423 founding)
stops.description: **Seongeup Folk Village (성읍민속마을)** is an inhabited Joseon-era village that preserves old Jeju stone walls, thatched-roof houses, narrow alleys, and traditional household layouts. Historically, the village served as the administrative center of Jeongui-hyeon, one of the main local districts of Joseon-era Jeju, and its heritage value is recognized as an Important Folklore Cultural Heritage site. Unlike a staged folk park, people still live here, so the visit must be respectful and quiet around private homes. The village is also known for its old zelkova and hackberry trees near the center, traditional Jeju household culture, stone grandfather statues, and local stories connected to village life.
highlights: **Founded 1423** by Sejong the Great — administrative center until **1914 (~500 years)**
**110 traditional structures** within original eupseong walled-town footprint
**Important Folklore Cultural Heritage of South Korea** (entire village + multiple buildings)
**Two ~600-year-old trees** at center: Saw-leaf Zelkova + Japanese Hackberry — **Natural Monuments**
Designed on **Joseon Korean feng shui (geomancy) principles** — typical county-capital plan
**'Dae Jang Geum' (MBC 2003–2004)** filming location — international K-drama hit
**Omegisul (오메기술)** — rice-based Jeju liquor brewed by single family for generations (free tasting)
Historical scars: **1826 fire** destroyed ~80 buildings; **1948 Jeju Uprising** damage
**FREE admission** — village still inhabited by ~250 families maintaining traditions

stops.name: Return to Jeju Cruise Terminal (Jeju Port — north)
stops.description: Return drive to the **Jeju International Passenger Terminal** (제주항 국제여객터미널) on Jeju City's northern coast. The route from East Jeju (Seongsan/Seongeup area) takes ~70–80 minutes via Highway 1132 (the coastal ring road) and Route 16, with one designated comfort stop. Drop-off is timed to deliver guests to the terminal **at least 60 minutes before your ship's all-aboard time**, traveler-fit the cruise industry's standard buffer for embarkation queues. The vehicle pulls into the **100-bus parking capacity lot**, guests disembark at the terminal entrance with luggage assistance from the driver/guide. If guests have early evening flights from Jeju International Airport, **Bus #100 connects directly from the cruise terminal to the airport** — the guide can advise on this option. The terminal interior offers convenience store, pharmacy, snack shop, souvenir shops with **tax-free items**, Jeju Bank ATM accepting overseas cards, currency exchange (typical hours 08:00–17:00), and SK Telecom T-mobile Wi-Fi rental counter — useful if guests want last-minute purchases or to reload mobile data before sailaway. **Free shuttle bus to Dongmun Traditional Market** (oldest/largest in Jeju City, ~10 min) is also available for any guests with extra time before all-aboard.

stops.name: Hallasan 1100 Altitude Wetland (Ramsar + UNESCO)
stops.category: Alpine Wetland — Ramsar Site + within Hallasan UNESCO World Heritage
stops.description: **Hallasan 1100 Altitude Wetland (한라산 1100고지 습지)** is a high-elevation alpine wetland system on the Hallasan plateau, located **at exactly 1,100 m above sea level** along **Route 1100 — the highest national highway in Korea** linking Jeju City to Seogwipo Jungmun. The site is a **Ramsar Wetland of International Importance (Site No. 1893, designated 12 October 2009)**, falling within the **Hallasan UNESCO World Heritage Site (2007)**, **Jeju Island UNESCO Biosphere Reserve (2002)**, and **Hallasan National Park (1970)** — making this one of the few sites globally with **four overlapping international/national designations**. The wetland comprises **16+ discontinuous freshwater marshes and pools** spread across a low-gradient plateau section that captures and retains rainwater, forming **small permanent lakes and peat bogs** that support diverse floristic assemblages with significant Jeju endemism. Recorded biodiversity includes **3 threatened bird species and 1 threatened plant species within Korea**, plus mammal, reptile, and insect species endemic to Jeju Island. The site received **900,000 visitors in 2008** (Mt Halla Visitor Centre data). Visitor experience: a **1-km wooden boardwalk loop** with observation decks, interpretive signage, and a small natural-history exhibit at the trailhead. **Autumn foliage (October–November)** and **winter snow scenes (December–February)** are the most photographed seasons; **spring + summer** offer wetland flora and endemic species visibility. **FREE admission**; adjacent **1100 Rest Area (1100 휴게소)** provides indoor restrooms + cafe + small souvenir shop. **Cultural and religious importance** to local communities is documented in the Ramsar listing — historical shaman pilgrimages tied to mountain-water spirits.

stops.name: Daepo Jusangjeolli Cliff
stops.description: **Daepo Jusangjeolli Cliff (대포 주상절리)** is one of Jeju's clearest examples of columnar basalt along the coast. Lava from Hallasan-area volcanic activity cooled and contracted into tightly packed polygonal stone columns, creating the dramatic cliff line now viewed from a safe seaside deck. The columns rise above the waves on the Jungmun coast and are especially photogenic when the sea is active. The visit is short, high-impact, and easier than a long coastal hike, making it a strong stop for mixed-age groups. Walking is mostly on maintained paths and viewing platforms, but wind and sea spray can vary by weather and tide.
highlights: **Natural Monument No. 443** — designated **2005-01-06**, the most recent Jeju coastal NM
Columns rise **30–40 m**, packed in **5- to 7-sided polygons** (mostly hexagons) along **1 km** of coastline
Formation: **Hallasan flank lava cooled in the sea ~250,000–140,000 years ago**, contracting perpendicular to cooling face
**East Asia's premier columnar-jointing site** — peer to Giant's Causeway, Devils Postpile, Fingal's Cave
Wave-spray photos may be possible when the tide and wind conditions are right
**0.8 km boardwalk loop**, ~15 min walk — barrier-free deck, wheelchair/stroller accessible
Part of **Jeju Global Geopark (UNESCO 2010-10)** — 13 island-wide geosites
Admission **₩2,000 adult / ₩1,000 youth / FREE under 7 + over 65**
Hallasan summit visible to the north on clear days, **directly downstream** geologically
timeUsed: Parking + ticket purchase + entrance — ~5 min
Walk to first viewing platform — ~5 min
Cliff-edge column viewing + photos — ~15 min
Continue boardwalk loop — ~10 min
Return to coach — ~10 min
stops.whyOnRoute: **Daepo Jusangjeolli Cliff is a compact, high-impact geology stop.** The viewing deck gives guests a clear look at Jeju's columnar basalt cliffs without a long hike, and the site fits naturally after Hallasan 1100 Highland before the route continues toward Seogwipo. The guide may adjust photo time depending on wind, tide, and crowd levels.

stops.name: Cheonjiyeon Falls (Seogwipo old-town waterfall)
stops.description: **Cheonjiyeon Falls** (천지연 폭포 — literally 'Sky-meeting-Land Falls') is the closer-to-port of Seogwipo's two famous waterfalls and is **NOT to be confused with Cheonjeyeon Falls** (천제연 — the three-tier 'Pond of the Heavenly Emperor' falls in Jungmun, ~15 min west). The single-tier cascade plunges **22 m / 12 m wide into a 20 m-deep emerald pool** at the end of a **gentle 1 km riverside trail** (~15 min one-way, paved and stroller-friendly) that follows a subtropical gorge dense with **rare plants**: **Damselfly Tree** (Natural Monument No. 163), **Crepidomanes makinoi** ferns clinging to the basalt walls, and dozens of endemic species protected as **Natural Monument No. 379** (the falls + surrounding habitat ensemble). The pool below is the protected habitat of the **Mutae eel** (마부태 / 무태 — *Anguilla marmorata*, the marbled eel), itself **Natural Monument No. 27** — a nocturnal species whose movement under nighttime illumination is one of Cheonjiyeon's signature scenes. Admission is **₩2,000 adults / ₩1,000 children** (one of Jeju's most affordable nature stops); operating hours **09:00–22:00 (last entry 21:30)** — the **after-dark illumination program 19:00–22:00** is unique among Jeju waterfalls and turns the gorge walls + falls into an emerald-jade light show, often paired with cruise overnight stays. The trailhead is **a 5-minute walk from Olle Maeil Market**, making the two stops a natural pairing on cruise day-trips originating from Gangjeong port. On clear days, **Munseom Island** (3 km offshore — Korea's first marine-park designation, 1994) is visible from the falls observation deck, and the basalt cliff faces of the gorge expose the same Hallasan-flank lava-flow stratigraphy as Daepo Jusangjeolli.
highlights: **Single-tier cascade 22 m × 12 m into 20 m-deep emerald pool** — Seogwipo's signature waterfall
**NOT Cheonjeyeon (천제연)** — different falls, different gorge, ~15 min west in Jungmun
**Natural Monument No. 379** (falls + endemic-flora habitat ensemble)
**Mutae eel (Natural Monument No. 27)** habitat — nocturnal marbled eel under illumination
**Damselfly Tree (Natural Monument No. 163)** + Crepidomanes makinoi ferns line the gorge
Gentle **1 km paved trail (~15 min)** — stroller/wheelchair friendly
**19:00–22:00 night illumination** — unique among Jeju waterfalls
**5-min walk from Olle Maeil Market** — natural pairing for south-route cruise days

stops.whyOnRoute: **Cheonjiyeon Falls adds an easy nature walk near Seogwipo.** The riverside path is gentler than many of Jeju's steeper coastal or mountain stops, so it works well after lunch. The waterfall, forested gorge, and pool give the south route a softer landscape contrast after Hallasan and Jusangjeolli. The guide also helps guests distinguish Cheonjiyeon Falls in Seogwipo from Cheonjeyeon Falls in Jungmun, which has a similar English spelling but is a different site.

stops.name: Olle Maeil Market (Seogwipo Maeil Olle Market)
stops.description: **Seogwipo Maeil Olle Market (서귀포매일올레시장)** is the largest traditional market in Seogwipo and a convenient food-and-shopping stop near Gangjeong Port. The market began in the early 1960s and now stretches through a covered arcade with many local food stalls, produce shops, seafood vendors, bakeries, and souvenir stands. Guests can look for Jeju citrus, black pork snacks, seafood, omegi rice cakes, local sweets, and simple gifts to take back to the ship. Most visitors enjoy it as a lively local-market walk rather than a formal shopping stop. Payment methods vary by stall, so carrying some Korean won is still recommended even though many larger shops accept cards.

walking_notes: Walking varies by port route option; both routes include moderate walking with optional climbs.
Jeju port (east coast) route option: Seongsan Ilchulbong rim climb is the steepest section (optional).
Gangjeong port route option: Hallasan 1100 boardwalk is easy; Cheonjiyeon falls path is short.
Comfortable walking shoes recommended; bring layers for Hallasan 1100 (Gangjeong route option).

matching_metadata.source_of_truth_note: Runtime recommendation reads Supabase match_tours.matching_profile. Cruise products require user_port parameter for route option selection.

port_routing_split.previous_state: Single itineraryStops representing one generic cruise route.
port_routing_split.rationale: Cruise ships call at different Jeju ports depending on route. Jeju Port (north) and Gangjeong Port (south, in Seogwipo) are 60+ km apart, so the day-trip route differs entirely. A single itinerary misrepresented the actual product served at each port.
matching_implications: When user_port is known (cruise booking context), score only the matched route option's poi_tags_in_variant / theme_tags_in_variant.
When user_port is unknown, fall back to matching_profile.poi_tags (union).
route option-level indoor_ratio_in_variant / weather_sensitivity_in_variant override matching_profile values when a specific port is selected.
downstream_actions: Frontend: add port selector UI (radio or chip) before itinerary renders. Default to 'Select your cruise port' empty state with both route option previews visible.
booking system: add port_id (text, nullable) column to bookings table; FK to a new cruise_ports reference table with rows for jeju_port, gangjeong_port.
traveler-fit algorithm: cruise products accept user_port as optional parameter. If provided, score route option-specific tags; otherwise score union from matching_profile.
Pricing review: validate whether bus / small group pricing differs by port — Gangjeong route includes Hallasan 1100 which adds drive time and toll vs Jeju Port's east-coast loop.
Existing bookings: backfill port_id where derivable from itinerary or pickup_base; flag rest as null for manual review.
Locale (ko): translate port_label, port_label_short, route_focus, and stop names/descriptions for Korean users.

derived_after_json_audit: unesco_fit=5 computed from canonicalized poi_tags union
rain_fit=2 because most route option stops are exposed and cruise return is fixed
young_kids_fit=2 because 8-hour cruise day with cruise schedule limit is long for under-6
mobility_friendly_fit=2 because Seongsan rim and Hallasan 1100 both require walking
route option-specific scoring overrides apply when user_port is known

score_rationale.unesco_fit: Both port variants include UNESCO World Heritage POIs (Seongsan or Hallasan-area).
score_rationale.weather_sensitivity: Cruise schedule is fixed — weather impact is structural.
score_rationale.value_for_money_fit: Bus format: highest value at lowest price tier.

locale_adaptation.note: Korean locale should translate port_label, port_label_short, and stop names/descriptions.

unesco_fit_per_variant.rule: Per documented unesco_fit scoring: 1 WHS = 4, 1 Geopark/Intangible = 2

matching_metadata.bus_fit_definition: bus_fit measures fit for LARGE-COACH format only. Mini-coach products score 1; large-coach products score 5.
matching_metadata.differentiation_from_sister: vehicle_type=coach (vs minicoach) + group_size 25-40 (vs 8-15) + value pricing tier
matching_metadata.port_variant_design: Both port variants (Jeju north / Gangjeong south) share the same product, dispatched per cruise dock

staticQuestions.question: 我要如何確認郵輪停靠哪個港口？
staticQuestions.answer: 您的郵輪訂位確認書上會列明濟州靠港地點。我們會在訂購時與您確認港口，並依此規劃當日行程。大多數主要郵輪公司停靠濟州港（北碼頭）；部分西歸浦一帶的靠港則使用江汀港。

staticQuestions.question: 我能趕回船上嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 可以。兩條路線方案均會在16:30至17:00之間返抵郵輪碼頭，並預留充裕時間應對交通延誤。我們會追蹤您的開船時間，並視情況調整行程節奏。

staticQuestions.question: 如果郵輪靠港地點臨時變更怎麼辦？
staticQuestions.answer: 請盡早通知我們——我們會隨即切換對應的路線方案。依港口調配路線正是本行程設有兩條路線選項而非單一路線的核心原因。

staticQuestions.question: 這個行程適合初次造訪濟州的旅客嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 非常適合——在均衡的一日行程中，涵蓋了該地區最具特色的景點。

staticQuestions.question: 步行路段困難嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 步行強度中等，部分路段較為活躍。

staticQuestions.question: 這個行程適合家庭出遊嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 適合孩子能承受全天移動行程的家庭。

staticQuestions.question: 年長者能否舒適地參加？
staticQuestions.answer: 僅建議體力充沛、能應對樓梯與不平坦地面的活躍樂齡人士參加。

staticQuestions.question: 建議的最低年齡是多少？
staticQuestions.answer: 最適合約6歲以上的兒童——需能適應全天行程節奏，並符合上述步行能力要求。

staticQuestions.question: 下雨天可以照常出發嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 部分景點為戶外露天環境；天氣過於濕冷時，可能縮短停留時間或更換替代景點。

staticQuestions.question: 午餐包含在內嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 行程中已規劃午餐休息時間。餐點本身是否包含在內，取決於您預訂的方案——請查閱費用包含項目清單。

staticQuestions.question: 我在哪裡上車？
staticQuestions.answer: 在您的飯店大廳或預訂區域內指定的接送地點。確切的接送時間將於前一天晚上通知確認。

itineraryStops.name: 濟州郵輪碼頭接送（濟州港——北岸）
itineraryStops.description: 您的司機與英語導遊將在**濟州國際旅客碼頭**（제주항 국제여객터미널，地址：濟州市臨港路191號——聯合國地點代碼 **KRCHA**）迎接您的一行人。這座北岸郵輪港口於 **2015年** 啟用，專門接待國際郵輪。碼頭設有兩個泊位——**主泊位（長360公尺、水深12公尺、容量14萬噸）**與**備用泊位（長306公尺、水深11.5公尺、容量8.5萬噸）**——每次僅可停靠**一艘郵輪**。接送時間訂於**船舶公告通關後30分鐘**，以應對多船同日抵港時的出入境延誤。司機持有英韓雙語名牌，並備有列印版行程卡（含各站時間及韓文地址，可供緊急搭乘計程車使用）與營運商24小時聯絡電話。車輛停放於緊鄰入境大廳、**可容納100輛巴士的停車場**。上車前，導遊將說明當日行程、集合時間、天氣狀況及任何港口日程調整。若您的旅客需要換匯（濟州銀行）、租用SIM卡／Wi-Fi，或使用多語言旅遊諮詢中心，導遊將全程陪同——以上服務皆設於碼頭內。根據**韓國文化體育觀光部**統計，**2024年共有81萬名郵輪旅客搭乘414艘船班抵港**——較前一年成長近三倍——因此多船同日靠港時，碼頭擁擠情況確實可能發生。
highlights: 直接於郵輪碼頭接送

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **濟州港（北岸）是14萬噸以下船舶的主要郵輪門戶**，亦是東線行程（城山／涉地可支／城邑）的天然出發點——三大核心景點均位於本碼頭60至80分鐘車程範圍內。對於**25至40人的巴士團**而言，通關後30分鐘的緩衝時間至關重要——大型團體下船速度較慢，多船同日抵港時出入境積壓亦屬常見。濟州港可容納100輛巴士的停車場，確保遊覽車可**直接停靠入境大廳門口**（無需等候接駁車），使全日行程保持緊湊。若您的郵輪停靠江汀港（南岸）而非本港，則應選擇**本行程的南線方案**——從江汀港至城山單程即需耗費70分鐘以上，將大幅壓縮行程時間。

itineraryStops.name: 城山日出峰
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO火山凝灰岩錐 — 日出峰（三重UNESCO認定）
itineraryStops.description: **城山日出峰（성산일출봉，「日出峰」）**是一座**擁有5,000年歷史的蘇特塞式水火山凝灰岩錐**——這是一種罕見的地貌，由水下玄武岩噴發衝破海面時形成：岩漿與海水接觸，蒸汽爆炸式噴發堆積形成**182公尺高、狀如城堡的火山口**邊緣，而非典型的熔岩錐。UNESCO正式稱之為**「蘇特塞式火山新生地貌的傑出典範」**。火山口**直徑600公尺、深90公尺**，四周環繞著**99塊嶙峋岩石**，當地傳說稱之為石冠。城山日出峰榮獲**三重UNESCO認定**：作為**「濟州火山島與熔岩洞窟」**遺產的一部分，於**2007年7月2日**列入**世界自然遺產**；**生物圈保護區（2002年）**；以及**世界地質公園（2010年10月）**——在韓國，僅漢拏山與之並列此殊榮。**18處二戰日本帝國軍洞穴**（1945年神風特攻隊自殺艇掩體）深鑿於面海的山腳基部。步道資訊：**單程0.8公里，爬升約140公尺，來回約60分鐘**，路面為寬闊石梯。**海女（해녀）表演**每日於火山口下方的礁石灣舉行，時間為**13:30及15:00**——濟州女性潛水員（2016年UNESCO非物質文化遺產）不借助水肺裝備徒手潛水。**2025年4月最新票價**：**成人₩5,000 / 青少年及兒童₩2,500 / 團體成人₩1,600**，7歲以下及65歲以上免費。山頂觀景台亦為**瀛洲十景**之一，是韓國最負盛名的**年度元旦日出朝聖**目的地——每年1月1日超過五萬名遊客於黎明前登頂，自1995年起設有市政新年日出慶典活動。從山頂清晰可見海岸外4公里的**牛島（우도，「牛島」）**——韓國最大的次生火山島，可由**城山港乘15分鐘渡輪**（來回₩10,500）前往，供行程有餘裕的旅客另作安排。

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **城山日出峰是東線行程的主要自然亮點。**此凝灰岩錐是濟州UNESCO世界自然遺產景觀的一部分，讓郵輪旅客對濟州火山海岸線留下深刻的第一印象。此站安排在行程前段，因為它是濟州最具代表性的景點之一，且火山口邊緣步道可根據旅客體能及天氣狀況彈性調整。不想攀登的旅客可在山腳區域欣賞海景及拍攝照片，其他人則繼續前往火山口觀景點。

itineraryStops.name: 涉地可支（東岬角）
itineraryStops.category: 火山岬角 — 安藤忠雄建築 + 防斗浦燈塔 + 影視拍攝地
itineraryStops.description: **涉地可支（섭지코지）**是位於濟州島東海岸的一處狹長火山岬角。在濟州方言中，**코지（koji）**意指岬角或海岬，此地以開闊的海景、平緩的海岸步道、白色燈塔，以及回望城山日出峰的廣闊視野著稱。步道景色優美，無需大量攀爬，非常適合希望拍攝精彩照片、享受新鮮海風，又不想整天攀爬的郵輪旅客。視時間安排，旅客或可欣賞玻璃屋區域及柳民藝術博物館外觀，兩者均與建築師安藤忠雄相關。此次參觀保持彈性，因為風雨、展館開放時間及團體步行速度均可能影響岬角的遊覽範圍。
highlights: 海岸懸崖步道

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **涉地可支為城山行程後新增的輕鬆海岸步道路段。** 此海岬讓旅客從另一個角度欣賞濟州東部風光：海蝕崖、燈塔景致、開闊草地，以及海岸線旁的現代建築。安排於此處是因為鄰近城山，可在午餐前有效率地完成上午路線。此站景色優美且具彈性，導遊可視風力、天氣及郵輪當日行程安排，靈活調整步行時間長短。

itineraryStops.name: 午餐（濟州東部 — 當地海鮮或黑豬肉）
itineraryStops.description: **濟州東部午餐站**，於城山／城邑／表善地區由導遊精選的在地餐廳用餐，配合郵輪一日遊的時程安排。**兩種常見選擇**，依團體喜好及餐廳空位決定：**(1) 濟州黑豬肉烤肉**（제주 흑돼지）— 濟州的代表性食材，原生黑豬於2015年獲指定為**韓國天然紀念物**，五花肉以炭火於桌邊現烤，佐以傳統小菜享用；或 **(2) 갈치조림（白帶魚燉蘿蔔）**（以醬油辣椒醬燉煮）— 濟州東部著名海鮮料理，食材取自城山及城邑一帶港口的每日漁獲。素食選項在大多數餐廳可事先告知安排（導遊會提前約30分鐘向餐廳確認）。午餐時間約為 **60分鐘**，符合郵輪一日遊緊湊的時程安排（全日行程通常為靠港至返港共8小時）。飲料方面：大麥茶（免費）、當地**濟州啤酒**（約₩6,000）或**漢拿山燒酒**（濟州代表性蒸餾酒，約₩5,000）。輪替餐廳均接受**主要信用卡（Visa／Mastercard）及韓圜現金**；郵輪旅遊合作餐廳均標配英中雙語圖文菜單。午餐**不含**於標準郵輪旅遊費用中 — 個人自付費用通常為 **₩15,000–25,000**（依餐點而異）；預訂時可選購含餐升級方案。
highlights: 傳統濟州料理

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **午餐安排在東線行程的中段**，以便在城山及涉地可支之後保持全天行程效率。位於城山、城邑或表善地區的餐廳，能讓行程更順暢地銜接至下午的村落景點，無需繞遠路。導遊將根據團體人數、餐廳營業狀況及時間安排，選擇合適的當地餐廳。若郵輪通關、交通或上午拍照所花時間比預期長，這段午餐時間也可作為實際緩衝。

itineraryStops.name: 城邑民俗村
itineraryStops.category: 朝鮮時代邑城古鎮——重要民俗文化遺產（1423 年建立）
itineraryStops.description: **城邑民俗村（성읍민속마을）** 是一座有人居住的朝鮮時代村落，保存了古老的濟州石牆、茅草屋頂房屋、狹窄的巷弄以及傳統家居格局。歷史上，此村曾是朝鮮時代濟州主要地方行政區之一——旌義縣的行政中心，其遺產價值已被認定為重要民俗文化遺產。與人工展演性的民俗公園不同，這裡仍有居民實際居住，因此參觀時必須保持尊重與安靜，切勿打擾私人住宅。村內亦以中央廣場附近的古老榉樹與朴樹、傳統濟州家居文化、石頭爺爺雕像，以及與村落生活相關的民間故事而聞名。
highlights: 傳統茅草屋頂房屋
石牆文化遺產

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **城邑民俗村是東線行程的主要文化亮點。** 它以濟州的人文歷史平衡了上午的火山地貌：石牆、茅草屋、村落樹木、古老街巷與在地故事。平坦的村落格局非常適合旅遊車團體，讓旅客在返回港口前，能以更悠閒的方式體驗傳統濟州生活。

itineraryStops.name: 返回濟州郵輪碼頭（濟州港——北部）
itineraryStops.description: 返回位於濟州市北岸的**濟州國際旅客碼頭**（제주항 국제여객터미널）。從濟州東部（城山／城邑地區）出發，沿1132號公路（沿海環形公路）及16號公路行駛，車程約70至80分鐘，途中設有一處指定休息站。下車時間經精確安排，確保旅客於所搭乘船隻全員登船時間的**至少60分鐘前**抵達碼頭，符合郵輪業登船排隊的標準緩衝規範。車輛駛入**可容納100輛巴士的停車場**，旅客在司機／導遊協助提取行李的情況下於碼頭入口處下車。若旅客需搭乘當晚從濟州國際機場出發的航班，**100號公車可直接從郵輪碼頭前往機場**——導遊可就此選項提供建議。碼頭內設有便利商店、藥局、輕食店、紀念品店（提供**免稅商品**）、接受海外卡的濟州銀行ATM、外幣兌換服務（一般營業時間為08:00至17:00），以及SK電信T-mobile Wi-Fi租借櫃檯，方便旅客在出航前進行最後採購或補充手機流量。此外，前往**東門傳統市場**（濟州市歷史最悠久、規模最大的市場，車程約10分鐘）的**免費接駁巴士**亦有提供，供全員登船前仍有餘裕時間的旅客使用。

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **返程車程已依郵輪時刻進行規劃。**從城邑與城山地區返回濟州港的車程，視交通狀況及路況約需70至80分鐘，因此路線安排將在船隻全員登船時間前預留充裕緩衝時間後再從東岸出發。導遊將全程掌控下午的行程進度，並視需要彈性調整如廁或拍照時間，確保返程順暢無虞。

staticQuestions.question: 我如何知道我的郵輪停靠哪個港口？
staticQuestions.answer: 您的郵輪預訂確認書上會列明濟州港口。我們會在預訂時與您確認停靠港口，並據此安排當天的行程路線。大多數主要郵輪公司停靠濟州港（北部碼頭）；部分西歸浦地區的停靠則使用江汀港。

staticQuestions.question: 我能及時返回船上嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 是的。兩條路線選項均可在16:30至17:00之間返回郵輪碼頭，並預留緩衝時間應對交通延誤。我們會追蹤您的出港時間，並在情況有變時隨時調整行程節奏。

staticQuestions.question: 如果我的郵輪停靠港口發生變更，該怎麼辦？
staticQuestions.answer: 請盡早通知我們——我們會據此切換對應的路線選項。港口路由正是這個行程設有兩條路線選項而非一條的原因。

staticQuestions.question: 這對初次造訪濟州的遊客來說是個好選擇嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 是的——它在一天均衡的行程中涵蓋了該地區最具特色的景點。

staticQuestions.question: 步行難度高嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 步行強度適中，部分路段較為費力。

staticQuestions.question: 這個行程適合家庭出遊嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 適合孩子能夠應付全天活動的家庭。

staticQuestions.question: 年長者可以舒適地參加嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 僅建議行動力強、能應對樓梯及不平坦地面的年長者參加。

staticQuestions.question: 建議的最低參加年齡是多少？
staticQuestions.answer: 最適合6歲以上的兒童——需能適應全天行程節奏及上述步行條件。

staticQuestions.question: 雨天可以進行此行程嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 部分景點為露天場所；天氣過於潮濕時，可能縮短停留時間或替換景點。

staticQuestions.question: 含午餐嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 行程路線中已安排午餐休息時間。餐食本身是否包含取決於所訂方案——請查閱費用包含項目清單。

staticQuestions.question: 我在哪裡上車？
staticQuestions.answer: 從您的飯店大廳或訂位區域的指定接送地點出發。確切接送時間將於前一天晚上確認。

v17_batch6_enhancement.description: Batch 6 deep enhancement — port-aware cruise shore excursion tour. Both variants (Jeju Port north + Gangjeong Port south) enhanced with 1,000+ char descriptions per stop; KB-backed _poi_meta on all attraction stops; matching_profile recalibrated to verified-fact 0-1 float scores; route option-level legacy 0-5 / 0-100 scale fields ALSO normalized to 0-1 float (Phase 1 unification completion for route option scope). Time-weighted indoor_ratio computed per route option per Fix 1 methodology.

_score_audit._methodology: 0-1 float scale per Phase 1 standard. route option-level legacy 0-5/0-100 scale fields ALSO normalized to 0-1 float in this batch (previously the schema unification stopped at top-level matching_profile). Time-weighted indoor_ratio per Fix 1 — computed PER route option then aggregated. Top-level matching_profile uses cross-route option averages weighted toward Gangjeong (the more common 2025+ docking).

unesco_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED HIGH — both variants hit major UNESCO sites. route option 0 (Jeju Port): Seongsan Ilchulbong = TRIPLE UNESCO (World Natural Heritage 2007 + Biosphere Reserve + Global Geopark) + Haenyeo ICH demos. route option 1 (Gangjeong): Hallasan UNESCO World Heritage + Biosphere Reserve + Jusangjeolli Global Geopark.
